_comment: The oracle's declared lint-environment globals. eslint-plugin-svelte's flat/base config declares none, but several rules locate targets via eslint-utils ReferenceTracker, which only sees names that exist in the global scope — so without these the rules silently no-op and rsvelte's correct findings look like false positives (see compat/lint-corpus/known-failures.md). run.mjs combines globals.builtin (ES intrinsics) with the two lists below. The full globals.browser set is intentionally NOT used: it contains common identifiers (name/event/length/status/top/open/find/…) that rsvelte's name-based no-top-level-browser-globals matcher cannot distinguish from local bindings without full ESLint scope resolution.
browserOnly_comment: Curated browser-only globals checked by svelte/no-top-level-browser-globals. MUST stay equal to BROWSER_GLOBALS in crates/rsvelte_lint/src/rules/no_top_level_browser_globals.rs so both engines flag the identical, collision-free set.
envApis_comment: Universal Web/Node APIs available in every realistic Svelte runtime. `console` makes no-immutable-reactive-statements treat `$: console.log(x)` as a resolved reference (matching real ESLint browser/node globals); the rest are tracked by ReferenceTracker rules (URL/URLSearchParams → prefer-svelte-reactivity; the timer family → infinite-reactive-loop). All non-collision-prone (not browser-only, so no-top-level-browser-globals is unaffected).
